title: Bart Vs Lisa Vs The Third Grade

jokes.text: Oh, I hate reality shows. / A year ago, you said they were the greatest thing that ever happened to us. / I've grown. You haven't.
jokes.punchline: His dismissive 'I've grown. You haven't' after Marge points out his hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his oblivious superiority complex when caught in obvious contradiction

jokes.text: So, Kevin, I hear you collect amusing postcards. / Uh, yeah, it all started when... / Touch the stove. - Aah!
jokes.setup: Host shows interest in guest's hobby
jokes.punchline: Immediate interruption with painful game show element
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of reality TV's cruelty masquerading as entertainment - excellent timing and structure

jokes.text: I can't take any more of these shows. If I wanted reality, I'd finally have this lump looked at.
jokes.punchline: Reveals his avoidance of actual reality (medical issues)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures Homer's character - avoids real problems while complaining about fake ones

jokes.text: Networks love reality shows because they don't have to pay writers or actors. / Stupid writers and actors, priced yourselves right out of the business. Nice going, geniuses.
jokes.punchline: Homer blames the victims of corporate cost-cutting
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the animation industry while staying in Homer's character - smart writing disguised as ignorance

jokes.text: Let's get one of those satellite dishes. Then we can stop suckling on the six-network teat. / Get back, honky cat. Those systems are too expensive.
jokes.setup: Homer's crude metaphor about network TV
jokes.punchline: Marge's dismissive 'honky cat' response
jokes.explanation: Crude humor with unexpected response - works but not exceptional

jokes.text: Come on, kids. Daddy knows a way to get some money with no risk. / [Cut to] And the winner by a nose is No Risk. Woo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Homer claims to know a no-risk money scheme
jokes.punchline: Cut reveals he literally bet on a horse named 'No Risk'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that works on multiple levels - Homer's literal interpretation and lucky gambling

jokes.text: Can you be home from 8 a.m. Monday morning through June? / No problem.
jokes.setup: Installer asks about ridiculously long installation window
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate acceptance of the absurd timeframe
jokes.explanation: Parodies cable installation windows and Homer's obliviousness - solid situational humor

jokes.text: Boy, that's jim-dandy roof candy. / I'd love to come over and watch that Church Channel. / I bet you would. / Oh, you'd win that bet.
jokes.setup: Ned admires the satellite dish with his characteristic vocabulary
jokes.punchline: His earnest response to Homer's sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Ned Flanders characterization - his oblivious earnestness deflates Homer's sarcasm

jokes.text: I'm spending all my money on religious pay-per-view. Or as I like to call it, 'pray-per-view.'
jokes.punchline: His proud pun combining 'pray' and 'pay-per-view'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Flandersy pun that he's proud of - captures his wholesome corniness

jokes.text: Damn your sparkling wordplay. And bless your humble home.
jokes.punchline: His conflicted curse-blessing combination
jokes.explanation: Homer's internal conflict with Ned perfectly captured - annoyance mixed with grudging respect

jokes.text: Oh, it's red wire to red wire. What idiot dreamed that up? / And the Lord said, 'Let there be crap.'
jokes.setup: Homer struggles with simple color-coded wiring
jokes.punchline: His biblical parody about the resulting bad TV
jokes.explanation: Homer's incompetence meets his crude humor - solid character moment

jokes.text: Cool. It's the NBC News feed. / Mm, feed.
jokes.punchline: Homer's food-focused interpretation of 'feed'
jokes.explanation: Simple Homer food fixation - functional but predictable

jokes.text: We'll be right back with a special report on soccer moms who hate soccer.
jokes.punchline: Contradictory demographic creates absurd news story
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire about manufactured controversy - good observational humor

jokes.text: Oh, Lord, I'm so fat, aah. [VOMITING]
jokes.punchline: Anchor's personal breakdown and vomiting
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that exposes TV personality facades - works but relies on shock value

jokes.text: Do you like my new shirt? It says, 'Reggae Hairstyle Rock 'r Roll.' Could I be more Japanese?
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical English phrase that sounds like bad translation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of Japanese pop culture appropriation and translation errors

jokes.text: You are the emperor of last year. Your comeback shames me.
jokes.punchline: Overly formal insult that sounds like bad translation
jokes.explanation: Continues the translation humor perfectly - sounds profound but meaningless

jokes.text: Coming up on The Clock Channel: 6:00. / Wait a minute, I saw this one.
jokes.setup: Announcer previews upcoming time on Clock Channel
jokes.punchline: Bart's complaint about reruns of time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept executed perfectly - Clock Channel reruns is wonderfully stupid

jokes.text: Hey, I don't have to study on the weekend. / It's Wednesday night. / Kids, kids, you're both right.
jokes.setup: Bart argues about not studying on weekend
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical mediation when it's actually Wednesday
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer parenting - avoiding conflict with nonsensical compromise

jokes.text: We're back with Who Wants to Marry an Internet Billionaire? Uh, no, millionaire. Oh, well, now he's broke.
jokes.punchline: Rapid downward revision of the bachelor's wealth
jokes.explanation: Smart satire of dot-com bubble and reality TV desperation - good escalation

jokes.text: I made a pie. / Put pie here. / Would you like some ice cream with that? / Me not pig.
jokes.punchline: Homer's caveman-like responses and defensive reaction
jokes.explanation: Homer's gluttony reducing him to primitive communication - solid character comedy

jokes.text: We now return to Robo Trouble. Oh, I'm sorry. I mean, Robot Rumble.
jokes.punchline: Correction reveals generic robot show naming
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of generic TV show titles - functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: Why are we fighting each other? Together we can defeat the humans and rule the Earth. / I agree. / Sucker.
jokes.punchline: Immediate betrayal after agreement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about robot solidarity followed by typical backstabbing

jokes.text: Homicidal robots. So like us.
jokes.setup: Robots betraying each other on TV
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual identification with violent robots
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly capturing human nature through Homer's casual observation

jokes.text: Bart, the test is in two hours and you haven't slept in a fortnight. / What's a fortnight? / You should know. It's on the test.
jokes.punchline: Reveals Bart's ignorance of test material
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Bart's unpreparedness through vocabulary confusion

jokes.text: No pressure, children. These test results will follow you for the rest of your life and beyond the grave.
jokes.punchline: Immediately adds maximum pressure with afterlife consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect teacher anxiety-inducing mixed message with absurd escalation

jokes.text: This test is boring. What else is on?
jokes.punchline: Treats test like TV channel surfing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart behavior - treating serious academic situation like entertainment

jokes.text: It's finally happened, Bart. You've lost your mind.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Lisa's clinical assessment of Bart's breakdown - good sibling dynamic

jokes.text: Now, let's raise the roof for the bland informative rap of M.C. Safety and the Caution Crew.
jokes.punchline: Describes safety rap as 'bland' while trying to hype it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - unaware of how unappealing his description sounds

jokes.text: I say a crosswalk, a cross-ity walk / And don't stop crossing / Till you're on the next block / First you look both ways / Then you walk not run / Obeying safety rules is acceptable fun
jokes.punchline: Painfully awkward safety lyrics trying to be hip
jokes.explanation: Classic educational cringe - adults trying to be cool for kids

jokes.text: Watch out, Beatles.
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic comparison to legendary band
jokes.explanation: Good Bart sarcasm - deflating adult pretensions with perfect timing

jokes.text: She's not so great. She got diarrhea when we went to Carlsbad Caverns.
jokes.setup: Lisa being praised for academic achievement
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude attempt to undermine her with embarrassing detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling jealousy - Bart's desperate attempt to embarrass Lisa publicly

jokes.text: What? I gotta be in the same class as her? / What? I gotta be in the same class as him?
jokes.setup: Both siblings being moved to third grade
jokes.punchline: Identical horrified reactions to being together
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamics - mutual horror at forced proximity

jokes.text: Hmm? They're gonna be in the same class as each other?
jokes.setup: Learning about Lisa and Bart's situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed, confused understanding
jokes.explanation: Homer's slow processing creating comedy through delayed reaction

jokes.text: An old Army buddy is visiting Mannix?
jokes.setup: Homer seemingly processing the school situation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually thinking about TV show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer misdirection - completely ignoring family crisis for TV trivia

jokes.text: Lisa comes to us from Miss Hoover, while Bart was taught by Mrs. Krabappel. Slut.
jokes.punchline: Casual slut-shaming of Krabappel
jokes.explanation: Shocking casual insult delivered matter-of-factly - good timing and shock value

jokes.text: A rooster sits on a roof facing north. It lays an egg. Which way does it roll? / Okay, the sun rises in the east, so the rooster would probably wanna lay it on the cool side... / Roosters don't lay eggs. They're boys.
jokes.punchline: Bart overthinks while Lisa knows the simple answer
jokes.explanation: Classic riddle highlighting character differences - Bart's overthinking vs Lisa's logic

jokes.text: Young man, you're not in this class. What are you doing here? / Laughing at jerks.
jokes.punchline: Nelson's honest admission of his bullying purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson honesty - no pretense about his bullying motivations

jokes.text: Quiet nerds burp only near school. / Dogs eat barf solely on Wednesday, Mabel.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly crude mnemonics
jokes.explanation: Bart's crude study methods contrasting with Lisa's academic approach

jokes.text: Bart: 'Dogs eat barf solely on Wednesday, Mabel.'
jokes.punchline: Another crude but functional memory aid
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the mnemonic gag. Less clever than the first one but maintains the joke

jokes.text: Bart: 'Canada's governors general: Clowns love haircuts, so should Lee Marvirs valet.'
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd memory aid
jokes.explanation: Third iteration weakens the gag. Running into diminishing returns territory

jokes.text: What's this weird mark next to my A? / That's an A-minus. / Mm, minus?
jokes.punchline: Her horror at receiving less than perfect grade
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa perfectionism - genuinely confused by imperfection

jokes.text: You did better than me? / Eh, I took this test last year. The answer key never changes. B-C-B-C-A-A-B-B-C-C-D. False. False. True. William Jennings Bryan.
jokes.punchline: Bart reveals his cheating method
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Lisa's merit-based worldview and Bart's corner-cutting

jokes.text: Young lady, in the third grade, we don't care for tattletales. / And we don't care for moaners either. / Sobbing only pushes Bart's grade higher.
jokes.punchline: Teacher progressively dismisses Lisa's legitimate concerns
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of teacher's dismissiveness and unfair grading system

jokes.text: Kids, look what I got you for your third-grade field trip. Matching fanny packs. Now your fannies match.
jokes.punchline: Her oblivious pride in embarrassing matching accessories
jokes.explanation: Classic mom embarrassment - well-meaning but tone-deaf to kids' social needs

jokes.text: Saying your buddy's name out loud is a security risk.
jokes.punchline: Treating school trip like military operation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of school safety paranoia

jokes.text: Punch buggy red, un. / Punch buggy white, un. / What's punch buggy? / When you see a Volkswagen Bug, you punch somebody and yell the color.
jokes.punchline: Game spreads through bus causing chaos
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of childhood car game leading to bus-wide violence

jokes.text: Punch buggy taupe. / Oh, that really hurt.
jokes.punchline: Obscure color knowledge causing real pain
jokes.explanation: Good detail with sophisticated color name adding to the absurdity

jokes.text: Now, children, if you look up at the capitol dome, you'll see a mural of our state bird, the pot-bellied sparrow, eating our state pasta, bowtie.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous state symbols that make no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist state symbols - pot-bellied sparrow and state pasta are wonderfully stupid

jokes.text: The Capital City Goofball? / That's right. To win, he spent 80 million from his own pocket.
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurd campaign spending by obvious joke candidate
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about money in politics and unqualified candidates

jokes.text: Homework on a field trip? What have you been huffing?
jokes.punchline: Bart's accusation of drug use for ridiculous assignment
jokes.explanation: Good Bart response - inappropriate but logically questioning adult decisions

jokes.text: He thinks he's really cool. But frankly, the other kids are starting to wake up and smell the cooties.
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about Bart to Marge
jokes.punchline: Mixing 'wake up and smell the coffee' with childish 'cooties'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that captures Lisa trying to sound mature while using kid language

jokes.text: When I get through with that flag, it's gonna be a Bart-mangled banner.
jokes.setup: Bart planning to sabotage Lisa's flag
jokes.punchline: Pun on Star Spangled Banner with his name
jokes.explanation: Good pun that fits Bart's destructive nature while being clever

jokes.text: That was my worst unfurling ever.
jokes.punchline: Governor's professional assessment of the disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response suggesting this is a recurring problem for him

jokes.text: You weren't on this field trip. How did you get here? / Biked.
jokes.setup: Nelson appears at field trip location
jokes.punchline: Simple, honest answer to logical question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson dedication to bullying - will bike across town for opportunity

jokes.text: Ah, the buddy system, foolproof. / Floor it, Otto.
jokes.setup: Teacher checking if anyone missing their buddy
jokes.punchline: Immediately leaves without checking if Bart and Lisa made it
jokes.explanation: Good irony about the failure of supposedly foolproof systems

jokes.text: Ha, ha, they left without you. / They left without you too, you idiot.
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out he's equally stranded
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa logic deflating Nelson's bullying with simple observation

jokes.text: We'll just circle around like those kids in The Blair Witch Project.
jokes.punchline: References horror movie about getting lost in woods
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that works but isn't exceptional - standard pop culture humor

jokes.text: It's called, Lisa is Stupid. / You die now.
jokes.setup: Bart claims a book changed his life
jokes.punchline: Reveals crude insult book, Lisa threatens violence
jokes.explanation: Good sibling antagonism - Bart's childish provocation meets Lisa's rage

jokes.text: Principal Skinner: 'There, there, Audrey. It's happened to all of us.' [about losing students]
jokes.punchline: Skinner treats child endangerment as common occupational hazard
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about institutional negligence. Perfect Skinner bureaucratic detachment

jokes.text: Why is it always the kids with the long names?
jokes.punchline: Complaining about difficulty writing long names on memorial
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about prioritizing convenience over tragedy - perfectly Willie

jokes.text: A parent who waives the right to sue says what? / What? / Got it.
jokes.punchline: Homer falls for obvious trap
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer gullibility meeting Skinner's bureaucratic opportunism

jokes.text: We're from the Learn to Fart state.
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces state name in crude way
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer mispronunciation humor - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Lisa's a very special little girl. And we'll spare no expense in finding her. / What about Bart? / We're looking.
jokes.punchline: Minimal effort promised for Bart
jokes.explanation: Good institutional bias humor - school values good student over troublemaker

jokes.text: Well, as far as nerdy little sisters go, you're the coolest.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that's still sweet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart affection - genuine but filtered through his perspective

jokes.text: One day, I was really bored and Dad had left a steamroller idling in the driveway...
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Homer leaving steamroller running
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Homer negligence - steamroller left idling is wonderfully excessive

jokes.text: Aah! The plastic casing from the tip of Bart's shoelace. / A mother knows.
jokes.punchline: Marge's maternal tracking abilities are absurdly specific
jokes.explanation: Good exaggeration of maternal intuition to absurd detective levels

jokes.text: That's how Grandpappy wound up in these parts. They was taking us to Capital City to see The Nutcracker. And I wandered away from the group, married a bear and I started up my family.
jokes.punchline: Escalates from field trip to bear marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist backstory - Nutcracker to bear marriage is perfectly weird

jokes.text: I told you, I ain't a bear. / Roar, roar, roar. / No one understands you, She-Bear.
jokes.punchline: Mountain man's patronizing dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of absurd family dynamic with relationship humor

jokes.text: We were going there anyway to pick up the new Spy magazine. / I'm sorry, they don't publish that anymore. / The world I grewed up in is gone.
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis over magazine cancellation
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash humor - isolation meeting cultural change

jokes.text: And, Maggie, you no longer have to live with the burden of replacing Bart and Lisa.
jokes.punchline: Revealing Maggie was expected to replace dead siblings
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parental expectations and replacement children

jokes.text: If this episode has taught us anything, it's that nothing works better than the status quo.
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on sitcom reset button
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on sitcom conventions delivered by authority figure

jokes.text: That's just sad.
jokes.punchline: Bart's commentary on the situation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective closer - Bart as voice of reason is amusing reversal

tmdb_overview: The Simpsons buy a satellite television, which Bart spends so much time watching that he does not study for an important achievement test. He scores so low that Principal Skinner demotes him to third grade, while Lisa does so well that she is moved up to third grade. The two are placed in the same class and become rivals. During a trip to Capital City, they get separated from their class and are forced to help each other in order to find their classmates.
